meaning: n.(束马鞍用的)肚带,<俗>紧握,<俚>容易做的,有把握的事情vt.系肚带,紧握住,抓牢
examples: The first question is a cinch.
He's a cinch to win the race.
It sounds difficult, but compared to full-time work it was a cinch.
Another touch gesture makes it a cinch to compare multiple photos with one another.
If you listened to Bob Dole and Clinton on Friday, it would almost seem a cinch.
